rationale: In the theory section, the authors discuss the cited article in detail, including its Study 6 finding that framing automation to support internal attribution increases positive attitudes. They use this evidence to justify their own attribution-based mechanism and to motivate their hypotheses and mediation tests. This is more than a passing mention, because the citation directly informs how the citing paper explains consumer reactions to algorithmic versus human decisions.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Leung et al. (2018) as prior automation evidence that attribution processes shape consumer evaluations. It uses that result as conceptual groundwork for its own attribution account of reactions to favorable versus unfavorable decisions made by algorithms versus humans.
